perturbation.original_text: Client will pay Excite these revenue share payments within thirty (30) days after the close of the financial quarter in which Client recognizes the Net Revenue on these transactions.
perturbation.changed_text: Client will pay Excite these revenue share payments within ninety (90) days after the close of the financial quarter in which Client recognizes the Net Revenue on these transactions.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the payment deadline for revenue share. Section 7(g) originally stated payments were due within 30 days, but it is now changed to 90 days creating confusion and uncertainty about when payments are actually due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All undisputed payments that have accrued prior to the termination or expiration of this Agreement will be payable in full within thirty (30) days thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline from 30 days to 90 days. The contradicted text specifies that all undisputed payments are due within 30 days. This creates a direct conflict regarding the payment timeframe, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable as it provides conflicting deadlines for the same type of payment. This is an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Excite guarantees to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may suspend (but not eliminate) its payments specified in Section 7 for a maximum of sixty (60) days (the "Make-Good Period) during which Excite will deliver the shortfall of such impressions.
perturbation.changed_text: Excite guarantees to deliver the annual impressions totals set forth in Exhibit C hereto. If Excite fails to deliver the indicated number of impressions required during any annual period, Client may suspend (but not eliminate) its payments specified in Section 7 for a maximum of thirty (30) days (the "Make-Good Period) during which Excite will deliver the shortfall of such impressions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the Make-Good Period from 60 to 30 days creates an in-text contradiction. Section 9(e) now states a 30-day Make-Good Period, conflicting with the expectations set in Exhibit C where the original 60 day delivery period was assured, and potentially making it impossible for Excite to meet impression guarantees within the reduced timeframe.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the 'Make-Good Period' from 60 to 30 days. This contradicts Exhibit C, which outlines annual impression delivery schedules and implicitly relies on the original 60-day period for fulfilling impression guarantees. By shortening the period, the changed text makes it potentially impossible to meet the impression totals outlined in Exhibit C within the new timeframe, creating a direct inconsistency and affecting the enforceability of the impression guarantees.
